summary: Comprehensive backend test suite for CHC Pro AI. 27/27 tests passing covering captcha, full registration + OTP + MFA flow, validation (password/NPI/EIN/captcha), admin approve/reject/audit/pending, login (success, wrong creds, pending-approval block, brute-force lockout at 5 attempts, lockout reset), token refresh, change-password, and the full coding workflow (create session, upload, process with OCR/PHI-purge/coding). Verified PHI redaction and expected medical codes (J18.9, I10, 85025, 71046) appear in coding_result.

minor.issue: Server silently lowercases email but does NOT return the normalized email in the success response. Clients relying on the input casing can be confused. Consider echoing back the stored email.
Lists only role='coder' pending users. Other pending roles (if ever added) would be hidden. Not breaking today but keep in mind.
In-memory captcha dict _captchas is wiped at 1000 entries with .clear() which could drop valid pending captchas of concurrent users under load.
CORS_ORIGINS='*' combined with allow_credentials=True is spec-invalid for browsers; for HIPAA-aware app, explicit origins should be enforced in prod.

action_items: Consider returning normalized email in /api/auth/register response for UX clarity.
Tighten CORS_ORIGINS to explicit list once frontend domains are finalized (prod HIPAA requirement).
Captcha store: persist in Mongo with TTL or Redis so it survives worker restarts and scales horizontally.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 874 lines — consider splitting routers (auth, admin, coding, settings) into separate modules for maintainability (>700 line guideline).
Captcha is stored in process-local dict _captchas; not safe for multi-worker deployments. Use Redis/Mongo-backed store with TTL.
seed_admin correctly syncs password when env changes — verified working.
bcrypt hashes verified via working verify_password (passlib). Hash format check not exposed, but login works.
Audit log correctly excludes PHI (only action/meta keys stored).
Email is lowercased on both register and login — consistent.
Session cap business rule (max 2) enforced at create time AND list time — good.
Raw file bytes purged after processing — good PHI minimization.
require_approved_user does not restrict by role; admin (approved=true) can also create coding sessions, which is intentional per problem statement.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@chcpro.ai / AdminPass@2025! ; Coder: coder@chcpro.ai / CoderPass@2025!
seed_data_creation: Tests created several TEST_* users (pending/approved/rejected) via the registration flow; seeded admin + coder are untouched. Created and processed one coding session for the coder containing synthetic PHI record.
context_for_next_testing_agent: backend_test.py is the canonical regression suite (27 tests, all pytest classes). It exercises every endpoint in /app/backend/server.py including the full registration + OTP + TOTP MFA flow using pyotp, admin approve/reject, brute-force lockout, and a full OCR/PHI/coding round-trip on an inline text record. If tests start failing, first check that admin (admin@chcpro.ai) and coder (coder@chcpro.ai) are still seeded & approved, and that SESSION_RETENTION_HOURS is >=1. Note server lower-cases all emails; always compare with .lower().
rca_of_the_issue: During initial run, TestAdmin.test_approve_reject_flow failed because the test compared the pending-list email (server-normalized to lowercase) with the original mixed-case test email. Root cause: server.py line 231 `email = str(data.email).lower()` normalizes to lowercase before insert, so clients must compare with .lower(). Fix applied in the test file (used .lower() on generated test emails). No server-side change required.
